practice.name: Problem 18 (2026 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many ways can 60 be written as a sum of at least two consecutive positive even integers?

practice.name: Problem 18 (2015 AMC 8)
practice.statement: The product of the two 2-digit numbers is even. What is the probability that both numbers are odd?

practice.name: Problem 17 (2005 AMC 10A)
practice.statement: In the five-sided star shown, the letters $A, B, C, D,$ and $E$ are replaced by the numbers $3, 5, 6, 7,$ and $9$, although not necessarily in this order. The sums of the numbers at the ends of the line segments $AB, BC, CD, DE,$ and $EA$ form an arithmetic sequence, although not necessarily in this order. What is the middle term of the sequence?

practice.name: Problem 23 (2023 AMC 10B)
practice.statement: The sum of $n$ terms of an arithmetic progression is $221$. If the first term is $a$ and the common difference is $d$, what is $a+d+n$?

practice.name: Problem 21 (2024 AMC 10A)
practice.statement: The numbers, in order, of each row and the numbers, in order, of each column of a $5 	imes 5$ array of integers form an arithmetic progression of length $5$. The numbers in positions $(5,5), (2,4), (4,3),$ and $(3,1)$ are $0, 48, 16,$ and $12$, respectively. What number is in position $(1,2)$?
